extraction.one_line_summary: GROVES Kaden timed his sprint perfectly on the uphill finish, coming around MILANO in the final 25 meters after extensive Alpecin-Deceuninck pacing.
tactical_patterns: Alpecin-Deceuninck controlled tempo entire stage without needing GROVES visible, conserving him completely for uphill finish at 350m left turn.
MILANO went from 100+ meters out on steep uphill, forced early by lack of leadout, nearly held off GROVES.
Sharp left turn at 350m to go became selection point; EF rider crashed, decreasing radius punished early positioning errors.
Break caught late enough that peloton sprint teams had minimal recovery time between catch and uphill finish drag.

rider_signals.GROVES Kaden: positive: demonstrated superior timing discipline and uphill sprint power, second Vuelta stage win confirms status as dominant sprinter here
rider_signals.MILANO: positive: showed unexpected form holding off GROVES until final 25m from 100m launch with no leadout support
rider_signals.POGACAR: neutral: visibly conserving energy by skipping intermediate sprints unlike Tour de France behavior, signaling GC focus shift
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: neutral: sitting at 31 seconds after TTT, no positioning effort shown in sprint stage
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: neutral: 37 seconds down at 10th overall, passive in sprint stage as expected
